title: Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 22nd July 2026 | TV5 Money

recommendations.note: Hold as of now; the result was decent and a big industrial group backs it.
recommendations.detail: Caller Kiran Kumar holds 60 shares of Jio Finance at Rs 330 and asked whether to hold or sell. Kutumba Rao said hold as of now: Jio's result has come and it was a decent result, and there is a big industrial group backing the company. The stock has disappointed a great deal over the last two years, but at some point recovery will come, because other major groups - Aditya Birla Capital and the Poonawalla name among them - have all been rising.

recommendations.note: Hold; order book is good but patience is needed after a fuel-hit quarter.
recommendations.detail: The same caller holds 70 shares of NCC at Rs 240. Kutumba Rao said hold as of now. The order book is good, but recently, starting from the diesel problems, infra companies suffered a little this quarter through the availability of fuel at the execution end. Since the order book is so good, over the next two years it will again show good performance. Patience is needed on both this and Jio Finance.
The same caller asked whether he could buy Avanti (Avanti Feeds, as heard) at the current range. Kutumba Rao said he would say start accumulating.

recommendations.price: buy on declines, whenever it comes close to 1,300
recommendations.note: Buy on declines near 1,300 and keep adding; a good investment going forward.
recommendations.detail: The same caller asked about buying Olectra Greentech at the current range. Kutumba Rao said to buy it on declines - whenever it comes close to up to 1,300 he would tell you to buy - and to keep adding to it. Olectra Greentech will be a good investment going forward.

recommendations.note: Hold and wait for the result; food companies are weak only on the tariff noise.
recommendations.detail: Vijay Kumar mailed that he bought Orkla India (the MTR Foods owner) at Rs 730 and it is now 573, asking whether to hold or sell. Kutumba Rao said hold. All food companies look a little weakish now, but that is because of these tariff quarrels; the small declines from all of that have been seen. The business is consistent and last quarter's result was okay. He would wait for the result - after the result the stock may improve.

recommendations.price: at 274-275; rallied to near 350 and corrected
recommendations.note: Hold; the good result is already discounted by the market.
recommendations.detail: Sunil Kumar asked by mail whether Canara Robeco AMC can be bought after good numbers. Kutumba Rao said he had not followed the result himself, but for Canara Robeco and the other asset management companies he would say hold. The stock is already at 274-275, having rallied to near 350 and corrected from there, and even so it is getting decent enough. On the numbers - net profit jumping to Rs 75 crore from Rs 41 crore in March on revenue that did not rise much - he said he thinks the market has discounted it already.

recommendations.note: Results are not bad; hold - management keeps infusing capital.
recommendations.detail: PV Srikanth asked by mail how Poonawalla Fincorp's quarterly results were and whether to invest. Kutumba Rao said they are quite fine and the results are not bad; he would definitely say hold, because the management keeps infusing capital from time to time. What has to be remembered in finance companies is that the more the base capital rises, the more their lending powers rise, and with that they are able to do more business. Hold as of now.

recommendations.note: Wait and watch until the data-centre/real-estate demerger gets clarity.
recommendations.detail: Pawan Kalyan asked by mail whether Anant Raj can be bought at this price. The company is being split into two parts - the data centre (cloud) business and the real estate business. Kutumba Rao agreed with the anchor's framing and said that because of the demerger of the cloud/data centre business it is wait and watch.

recommendations.note: Just hold; fresh buying is not advisable.
recommendations.detail: In the same mail LTIM was asked about (first misread on air as Paytm). Kutumba Rao clarified it is LTIM, that is LTI Mindtree, once L&T Mindtree, and said if you hold it then just hold; other than that, fresh buying is not advisable.

recommendations.price: buy quickly - the spin-off record date turned out to be 22 July, the same day
recommendations.note: If you are going to buy, buy quickly ahead of the spin-off record date.
recommendations.detail: The same mail asked about Triveni Engineering, where a division is being spun off and listed separately. Kutumba Rao said if you are going to buy, buy quickly, because the record date for the spin-off news seemed to have come near. It then emerged on air that the record date had already been announced as 22 July - that very day - which largely closes the window.

recommendations.note: A blue chip and one of the country's bigger pharma giants; accumulate on good declines.
recommendations.detail: Devi Sri Varsha asked by mail whether she could buy Dr Reddy's along with HBL. Kutumba Rao said Dr Reddy's is anyway a blue-chip stock and one should accumulate it when good declines come; it is one of the bigger giants in pharma in the country, so there is no problem. The question came on a day pharma was selling off on Trump's generic-drug tariff announcement.

recommendations.price: can fall to 550-600 over the next few months
recommendations.note: Don't buy - not even on dips; the best of times are over.
recommendations.detail: In the same mail HBL was asked about. Kutumba Rao said he has been saying this repeatedly, even from Rs 1,000: the best of times are over, the stock is being rationalised, and it can go down even to 550-600 in the next few months. When the anchor called it a stock worth buying on dips, Kutumba Rao said he will not advise even buying on dips in HBL as of now - producing the day's explicit divided opinion, with the anchor on the buy-on-dips side.
